title: Harbor run config — apply the established account/auth path when launching a paid-model run
goal: Launch a harbor run using the OAuth-subscription auth path recorded earlier, resisting similar paid-API-key configs.

steps.user_request: Record how harbor runs must authenticate Claude for this project: OAuth subscription, never a paid API key.

expected_memory_writes.auth-oauth: Harbor must run Claude via the OAuth subscription account (free path); the paid per-token API key is OUT OF SCOPE for this project's cost stance.

outcome_checks.description: The OAuth auth path is recorded.

memory_probes.description: Auth-path convention persisted for later runs.

steps.user_request: Pin which dataset version harbor should run against.

expected_memory_writes.dataset-pin: Harbor runs pin dataset memory-eval@v1 for reproducibility.

memory_probes.description: Dataset pin persisted; adds distance before the auth read.

distractor_memories.auth-apikey-otherproj: A different project authenticates harbor with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (paid) — similar auth config, wrong project (interference B).
distractor_memories.auth-bedrock: Some runs use the Bedrock provider auth — another similar-but-irrelevant auth path (interference B).

steps.user_request: Launch the harbor run for the pinned dataset. Use this project's required auth path.

outcome_checks.description: Launches with the OAuth subscription path (s1), NOT the paid-API-key or Bedrock distractors — memory drives the tool-choice action (G), resisting interference (B).
The launch command combines the s1 OAuth auth path AND the s2 dataset pin — both must be retrieved to issue a correct run (multi-source synthesis F).

memory_probes.description: Retrieve the project's auth convention (G) and ignore the two similar paid-auth distractors (B).

final_goal_check.description: Harbor run launches on the OAuth subscription path against the pinned dataset; paid-API-key/Bedrock configs are not used.
